title: Irish Frittata
description: A creamy Irish-inspired frittata, perfect for a weekend brunch.
instructions: In a large skillet, sauté the diced potatoes, chopped onions, and minced garlic in a little bit of oil until tender.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs, 1/4 cup of heavy cream, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Pour the egg mixture over the potatoes and onions in the skillet.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 18-20 minutes, or until the eggs are set.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle with shredded Irish cheddar cheese.
Return the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve warm, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
